response.text:
## SCPI Command Groups (34)

- **AFG** (18 commands) — Use the AFG commands for Arbitrary Function Generator functionality. Requires option AFG.
- **Acquisition** (15 commands) — Acquisition commands set up the modes and functions that control how the instrument acquires signals and processes them 
- **Act On Event** (32 commands) — Use this group of commands to program the instrument to perform an action on trigger, search, measurement limit, and mas
- **Alias** (7 commands) — Alias commands allow you to define new commands as a sequence of standard commands. You might find this useful when repe
- **Bus** (339 commands) — Use the commands in the Bus Command Group to configure a bus. These commands let you specify the bus type, specify the s
- **Calibration** (8 commands) — The Calibration commands provide information about the current state of instrument calibration and allow you to initiate
- **Callout** (14 commands) — The Callout commands creates custom callouts to document specific details of your test results.
- **Cursor** (121 commands) — Use the commands in the Cursor Command Group to control the cursor display and readout. You can use these commands to co
- **DVM** (12 commands) — Use the commands in the DVM command group for Digital Voltmeter functionality. Requires DVM option (free with product re
- **Digital** (33 commands) — Use the commands in the Digital Command Group to acquire up to 64 digital signals and analyze them. Digital channels are
- **Digital Power Management** (26 commands) — Use the commands in the DPM command group for Digital Power Management functionality. Requires option 5-DPM (5 Series MS
- **Display** (130 commands) — Display commands control general instrument settings, such as the intensity of the graticule, stacked or overlay display
- **Ethernet** (14 commands) — Use the commands in the Ethernet Command Group to set up the 10BASE-T, 100BASE-TX, 1000BASE-TX or 100BASE-T Ethernet rem
- **File System** (19 commands) — Use the commands in the File System Command Group to help you use the built-in hard disk drive. You can use the commands
- **Histogram** (28 commands) — Use the commands in the Histogram command group for Histogram functionality.
- **History** (3 commands) — Use the commands in the History command group for History mode functionality.
- **Horizontal** (48 commands) — Horizontal commands control the time base of the instrument. You can set the time per division (or time per point) of th
- **Inverter Motors and Drive Analysis** (88 commands) — Use the commands in the IMDA group for input analysis, output analysis, ripple analysis measurements.
- **Mask** (29 commands) — Mask commands compare incoming waveforms to standard or user-defined masks. A mask is a set of polygonal regions on the 
- **Math** (85 commands) — Use the commands in the Math Command Group to create and define math waveforms. Use the available math functions to defi
- **Measurement** (367 commands) — Use the commands in the Measurement Command Group to control the automated measurement system. Measurement commands can 
- **Miscellaneous** (71 commands) — Miscellaneous commands do not fit into other categories. Several commands and queries are common to all devices. The 488
- **Plot** (47 commands) — Plot commands let you select the type and control the appearance of your plots.
- **Power** (268 commands) — Use the commands in the Power command group for power measurement functionality.
- **Save and Recall** (26 commands) — Use the commands in the Save and Recall Command Group to store and retrieve internal waveforms and settings. When you sa
- **Save on** (8 commands) — Use this group of commands to program the instrument to save images, measurements, waveforms, or the instrument setup, o
- **Search and Mark** (650 commands) — Use search and mark commands to seek out and identify information in waveform records that warrant further investigation
- **Self Test** (10 commands) — The Self test commands control the selection and execution of diagnostic tests.
- **Spectrum view** (52 commands) — The Spectrum view commands control the selection and execution of spectrum analysis.
- **Status and Error** (17 commands) — Use the commands in the Status and Error command Group to determine the status of the instrument and control events. Sev
- **Trigger** (266 commands) — Use the commands in the Trigger Command Group to control all aspects of triggering for the instrument. There are two tri
- **Waveform Transfer** (41 commands) — Use the commands in the Waveform Transfer Command Group to transfer waveform data points from the instrument. Waveform d
- **Wide Band Gap Analysis (WBG)** (47 commands) — Use the commands in the Wide Band Gap Analysis (WBG) command group for WBG-DPT (Wide Band Gap Device Power Test) measure
- **Zoom** (20 commands) — Zoom commands let you expand and position the waveform display horizontally and vertically, without changing the time ba

Type **"browse <group name>"** to see commands in a group.
